ks1.objective: Hit each ball off the tee, run between stumps to score, and field as a team to limit the batter's runs.
successCriteria: Hits each of the 3 balls off the tee
Runs around the stumps to score 1 run each
Stays in the safety zone when not batting

lks2.objective: Add tactics — aim at scoring zones for bonus runs, or hit over the safety line for 6.
successCriteria: Picks an intent before each hit (zone vs distance)

uks2.objective: Master tactical batting under fielder pressure and run with awareness of fielders' progress.
successCriteria: Reads fielders' progress when deciding to stop running
Times the STOP call as a fielder for maximum advantage

space.description: Pitch layout (see diagram). Batter safety area on one side. 3 batting tees in a column, 6m apart, in front of the safety area. Set of stumps 18m away (behind the fielders). Width 10-12m. Cones mark the safety line ends and the scoring zones on the sides. Fielders line up behind the safety line at the far end.

tierOverrides.ks1: Use the lower end of distances (6m between tees, 18m to far stumps).
tierOverrides.uks2: Use the upper end of distances. Increase scoring zone challenge by narrowing the zones.

steps: Set up the pitch per the diagram. Team of 6, with at least 2 boys and 2 girls. One team bats, one fields.
The first batter hits each ball off the tee in turn. Fielders CANNOT move unless the ball is in the air available for a catch.
A catch CANNOT be taken in front of the safety line. If caught, fielders get +6 runs.
As each ball is hit, fielders shout 'ONE', 'TWO', 'THREE' to keep track.
Scoring on the hit: scoring zones = 2 runs; lands beyond the safety line = 6 runs.
Hits outside the cones at the safety-line ends must be REPLAYED.
Once all 3 balls are hit, the batter runs around the stumps. Each lap = 1 run.
After all 3 balls are hit, fielders can MOVE to reset the balls and tees.
Other batters stay in the safety zone and help count runs.
When tees and balls are reset, fielders run back to the safety line and shout 'STOP'.
Next batter takes their turn. After all batters, swap teams. Total scores added.

cues.trigger: before each hit off the tee
fielders shout after each ball is hit

errors.issue: Fielders move before the ball is in the air
errors.fix: Disallow the play; restart the ball

errors.issue: Catch attempted in front of the safety line
Batter hits outside the channel cones
errors.fix: Replay the ball; cue 'aim THEN swing'

progressions: Perfect the skill: increase the size of the scoring zones; use bigger balls.
Challenge: decrease the size of scoring zones; require batter to aim AT a zone rather than just hit it.
Try 'Rapid Fire' — reduced reset time between batters.
questions: Where's the best zone to aim for — and why?
How do you decide whether to go for a 6 or play it safe?
What's the fielders' fastest way to reset and shout STOP?

step.space: Wider boundaries make 6s easier; narrower zones make 2s harder
step.task: Aim AT a specific scoring zone vs just hit it
step.equipment: Bigger balls for KS1; standard tennis balls for LKS2+
step.people: Teams of 5 or 6 work fine — adjust safety zone capacity

base.showmanship: Whole-class involvement — non-batters count and cheer. Use the fielders' shouts of 'ONE / TWO / THREE' to build tempo. Praise tactical batting and quick, organised resets.
